name: Kishar
description: Kishar, in Babylonian mythology, is a primordial goddess representing the entire land, the solid earth. She is the daughter of Lahmu and Lahamu, the first pair of deities to emerge from Apsu and Tiamat, the primordial waters. Kishar is the sister and consort of Anshar, the primordial god representing the entire sky. Together, Kishar and Anshar are the parents of Anu, the god of the sky, and thus grandparents of all the other major gods in the Babylonian pantheon. Kishar's role is primarily as a passive figure, a foundational element in the creation narrative. She embodies the stability and physical reality upon which the cosmos is built. While not actively involved in the conflicts and dramas of the younger gods, her existence is essential for their world to exist. Kishar's importance lies in her representation of the earth as a fundamental and necessary component of the universe. She is a symbol of the grounding and material foundation upon which all of creation rests. Her name, meaning 'entirety of the earth,' encapsulates her role as the embodiment of the terrestrial realm. The creation myth, the Enuma Elish, highlights her lineage and her connection to the very origins of the cosmos, cementing her significance as a primordial deity.
subtitle: Primordial Goddess of the Earth

keyMyths.title: Birth from the Primordial Waters
keyMyths.description: Kishar is born from Lahmu and Lahamu, who themselves emerged from the mingling of Apsu and Tiamat, the primordial waters. This birth establishes her as a fundamental element of the cosmos.

keyMyths.title: Mother of Anu
keyMyths.description: Kishar, with her consort Anshar, gives birth to Anu, the sky god. This makes her the grandmother of many of the major Babylonian gods, highlighting her role in the divine lineage.

keyMyths.title: The Foundation of the World
keyMyths.description: Kishar represents the earth, the solid foundation upon which the world is built. Without Kishar, there would be no stable ground for the gods and humans to exist.

sources.author: Stephanie Dalley
